items.correct: Information the performer senses from their own body during/after movement.
items.why: Students reverse the meaning. Intrinsic = from WITHIN (kinesthetic awareness, feel); extrinsic = from OUTSIDE (coach feedback, video, statistics). Experienced performers rely more on intrinsic feedback.

Students lump all training together. Plyometrics specifically targets POWER - explosive jumps and bounds use rapid eccentric-then-concentric muscle action, training the stretch-shortening cycle for fast force production.

items.q: How long does the ATP-PC system last at maximum intensity?
items.why: Students overestimate the ATP-PC system. It provides instant energy but stores deplete in ~10 seconds (one sprint, one heavy lift). After that, glycolysis or aerobic systems take over.

items.q: Unfair treatment based on race is?
items.why: It's Discrimination. “Etiquette” is the classic near-miss here; knowing why it's wrong is worth as much as knowing the right answer.
Correct answer: Sportsmanship. “Hooliganism” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: How many 'protected characteristics' does the UK Equality Act 2010 list?
items.why: Exact answer: 9 characteristics. Estimation gets you near it; only the anchored number survives the exam hall.
